root_text.an5.235:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato āvāsiko bhikkhu gihīnaṁ anukampati.
root_text.an5.235:1.7: mahā kho pana bhikkhusaṅgho abhikkanto nānāverajjakā bhikkhū gihīnaṁ upasaṅkamitvā āroceti:
root_text.an5.235:1.8: ‘mahā kho, āvuso, bhikkhusaṅgho abhikkanto nānāverajjakā bhikkhū, karotha puññāni, samayo puññāni kātun’ti;
root_text.an5.235:1.9: yaṁ kho panassa bhojanaṁ denti lūkhaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā taṁ attanā paribhuñjati, saddhādeyyaṁ na vinipāteti.
root_text.an5.235:1.10: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato āvāsiko bhikkhu gihīnaṁ anukampatī”ti.

translation_text.an5.235:1.1: “Mendicants, a resident mendicant with five qualities shows sympathy to the lay people.
translation_text.an5.235:1.3: They encourage them in higher ethics.
translation_text.an5.235:1.4: They equip them to see the truth of the teachings.
translation_text.an5.235:1.5: When they are sick, they go to them and prompt their mindfulness, saying:
translation_text.an5.235:1.6: ‘Establish your mindfulness, good fellows, in what is worthy.’
translation_text.an5.235:1.7: When a large mendicant Saṅgha is arriving with mendicants from abroad, they go to the lay people and announce:
translation_text.an5.235:1.8: ‘A large mendicant Saṅgha is arriving with mendicants from abroad. Make merit! Now is the time to make merit!’
translation_text.an5.235:1.9: And they eat whatever food they give them, coarse or fine, not wasting a gift given in faith.
translation_text.an5.235:1.10: A resident mendicant with these five qualities shows sympathy to the lay people.”
